title: Capture of Kalast
content:

Corvette/Legends|''Marauder''-class corvettes]] *[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] *[[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Moderate damages to the fleet |casual2=*Heavy damage to the fleet, ''Avenger'' Re-captured by Empire *Kalast captured |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|You'll have to catch me first.|Moff Kalast|}} The final '''battle''' in a long search for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] traitor [[Kalast]] saw the Empire capture the traitorous [[Moff/Legends|Moff]]. Kalast has stolen datacodes for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]], which had enough power to destroy entire worlds, for which the Empire sought his capture and punishment. ==Prelude== The search started on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], where Kalast was commanding a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] outpost. He escaped from the destruction of the base due to a miscalculation made by a commanding [[Unidentified Imperial commander|Field Commander]]. After putting down the [[Invasion of Fondor]] that occurred during the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army's]] absence from the planet, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] hired [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to take down the defenses around [[Ilum/Legends|Ilum]], where native [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] supplied Kalast with weapons. After gathering information from the Pirate leader, the Empire attacked a Rebel [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]] support unit during a [[Battle at Jabiim|battle]] at [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]], where information was gathered that Kalast owned a Rebel estate over [[Atzerri/Legends|Atzerri]]. Upon the [[Arrest over Atzerri]], the rogue Imperial fled. Darth Vader and [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] started a series of scouting missions across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] with a fleet including [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruisers]] to capture Kalast once and for all. ==The Hunt== The [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] engaged the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Alliance Fleet]] in a string of battles around the galaxy, from [[Shola/Legends|Shola]] to [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], destroying several [[Alliance Space Station]]s in a determined attempt to find him. They were able to pin Kalast down several times, but failed to effect his capture. After several battles, the final battle commenced which would bring the search for Kalast to an end. ==The capture== {{Quote|Sir, we're having trouble apprehending Moff Kalast. His Star Destroyer is one of the newer models, and we've been unable to successfully engage him before he enters hyperspace.''"
"''Indeed. This seems to be an excellent time to test our newest weapon. I will see to it that an Interdictor Cruiser is placed at your disposal on Coruscant. We shall see how elusive his Star Destroyer is… .|An Imperial Officer and Grand Moff Tarkin|}} Darth Vader found one of Kalast's last remaining strongholds. The [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruisers]] were activated, preventing his escape. As the fleet exchanged fire, combat ships ambushed Kalast's flagship, the ''Avenger'', and destroyed its shields and other weak points. The vessel thus crippled, an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial-I''-class Star Destroyer]] swooped in and caught Kalast in its tractor beam, taking the ''Avenger'' back intact as well. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Moff Kalast was most forthcoming… just before the end. It seems, Tarkin, that the Bothans have chosen a side in this conflict.|Emperor Palpatine|}} The capture of Kalast would lead to the obtainment of key Rebel information, including the details on the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] aiding [[Kalast]] in his spying, which would lead to [[Mission to Bothawui|many Bothans dying]] because of this information. Regardless, the damage was done, and Kalast's efforts ultimately were contributory towards the Rebels [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroying]] the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. ==Behind the scenes== This battle was the tenth mission of the Imperial campaign in the [[2006]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''. The actual locations and number of intermittent skirmishes can vary widely based on how many systems the Imperial player holds at this point in the campaign. If, for example, the Imperial held every system save for [[Atzerri/Legends|Atzerri]], only a single battle over the same system would be required. On the other hand, if several systems were left under Rebel control, catching Kalast would prove very difficult as he could jump between several systems, perhaps very far apart. If, however, the Empire has already conquered every available planet, a bug in the game happens: Kalast's fleet appears in orbit of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and the Imperial space station there changes to rebel control, making the fight against him difficult if it is of high level. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW 1 BBY 2}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht zur Gefangennahme Kalasts#legends |es=Captura de Kalast }} [[Category:Battles of the Galactic Civil War|Kalast]]
